news.title: How World Cup 2026 proved football has remarkable ability to cure all ills
news.lede: The trophy safely on display at the Spanish FA headquarters, England’s wounds no longer so raw, and the annual football calendar now into its miniscule vacuum, you’re left to reflect on the past six weeks and ponder whether the 2026 edition in the United States, Canada and Mexico was a resounding success or an unmitigated flop. This one really could have gone either way. The expansion from 32 teams to 48 had the potential for greater drama but also the risk of suffocating, of making the World Cup the one thing it should never be: tedious. Would North America buy into soccer in a way we hadn’t seen at USA 1994?

news.title: Best of the 2026 World Cup - Football Extra's team of the tournament
news.lede: Some picks are obvious - Rodri had to be in, and most of the Spanish defence too - but who else makes Football Extra's World Cup team of the tournament? Don't worry, it's not just a Spain XI. Find out below - and send us your thoughts to football.extra@bbc.com. There is of course an official award for the 'best' keeper, with Spain's Unai Simon the winner.

news.title: Why many Black Americans were rooting for Argentina to lose the World Cup | Etan Thomas
news.lede: Many Black Americans were not cheering Spain’s complicated history. They were rooting against the racism, politics and silence associated with Argentina. There are moments in sports when you realize people aren’t just watching the score any more. They’re watching who speaks.

news.title: 64 teams, six host countries: What could the 2030 World Cup look like?
news.lede: Infantino has opened the door to another World Cup expansion, with Fifa vice president Alejandro Dominguez in favour of it as early as the 2030 edition - but what would a 64-team tournament actually look like? From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging. At such a critical moment in US history, we need reporters on the ground.

news.title: Thames Valley Police record 73 World Cup incidents and make 12 arrests
news.lede: A police force recorded 73 football-related incidents and made 12 arrests during the World Cup, with more than half occurring around England's quarter-final and semi-final matches. Thames Valley Police (TVP) said alcohol-related disorder and anti-social behaviour were among the main issues encountered during the tournament. It added that it responded to 12 domestic abuse incidents linked to the competition, accounting for 16% of all football-related incidents. "While most fans celebrated responsibly, we took action where behaviour crossed the line," said Supt Ade Hall from TVP.

news.title: Fan group launch stinging attack on Fifa over 'World Cup that failed supporters' with 'serious concerns' over next hosts
news.lede: A FOOTBALL fan group has slammed Fifa for the 2026 World Cup and are already worried about what’s to come in 2030. Football Supporters Europe have taken aim at Fifa president Gianni Infantino, US president Donald Trump and Fifa as a whole in a statement. It reads: “The 2026 World Cup should have been a celebration of the game and the supporters who give it life. “Instead, it has too often become a case study in what is so deeply wrong with football today.”

news.title: World Cup becoming a 64-team tournament would create logistical challenges - so can Gianni Infantino's idea work?
news.lede: This year's World Cup is finally over - and after 48 teams took part, it was a long, long tournament. But just as we bid to say goodbye to the 2026 edition, this competition may be getting even bigger. FIFA president Gianni Infantino says any further expansion of the World Cup for 2030 - where six countries are set to host the tournament - ‌will be discussed now the 2026 World Cup has finished. And one solution FIFA are discussing is a 64-team tournament.

news.title: World Cup changes our readers would make: Ban offsides in the area? Timeouts? Shrink penalty box?
news.lede: Be specific on stoppage time? Replace a red-carded player? Subscribers have their say...
